# Figure description: скв_388.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_388.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **well log / borehole geophysical log** (каротажная диаграмма) — a composite wireline log typical of Soviet-era exploration drilling. It appears to be a long, vertically oriented strip log combining multiple geophysical curves recorded along the borehole axis, with accompanying lithological and stratigraphic columns on the left and right margins. The format is characteristic of Soviet промысловая геофизика (well logging) practice from the 1970s–1980s.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single borehole (скважина)** — the title at the top appears to read **"скв. Оксановский"** (Borehole Oksanovsky), suggesting it is named after a local geographic feature or prospect. The log extends over a considerable depth interval, likely several hundred metres based on the length of the strip and the density of the depth grid lines. The borehole appears to be located within the northern Kazakhstan survey area covered by map sheets **M-42 and/or N-42**.

The log includes what appears to be:
- A **lithological column** (left margin) with rock type symbols
- **Stratigraphic/formation assignments** in the far-left columns
- **Multiple geophysical curves** in the central panels (likely electrical resistivity, spontaneous potential, and/or gamma-ray logs)
- A **right-side margin** with additional annotations, possibly porosity, lithological descriptions, or drilling parameters

At the very bottom of the strip, a section labelled **"КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ"** (Control Curves) is visible — a standard Soviet logging practice showing calibration or repeat sections of the log curves.

---

## Key Content

### Geophysical Curves
The central portion of the log shows **two to three main curve tracks**:
- The **left track** displays a curve with high-frequency, moderate-amplitude oscillations throughout most of the log — consistent with a **КС (Кажущееся Сопротивление / apparent resistivity)** or **ПС (Потенциал Самополяризации / spontaneous potential)** curve
- The **central/right track** shows a curve with notably **higher amplitude excursions**, particularly in the upper-to-middle portions of the log, suggesting zones of contrasting lithology (e.g., sandstone vs. shale, or mineralised intervals). Several prominent spikes or peaks are visible
- There is a **transition zone** roughly in the middle depth range where the left curve flattens considerably and then resumes, possibly indicating a lithological break, casing shoe, or drilling fluid change
- The **lower portion** of the log shows reduced amplitude oscillations in both tracks, possibly reflecting more homogeneous lithology at depth

### Lithological Column
The left-side lithological column uses standard Soviet hatching symbols indicating alternating intervals of:
- Fine-grained clastic rocks (shales/mudstones — horizontal lines)
- Sandstone or siltstone intervals (dotted or stippled patterns)
- Possibly carbonate or crystalline basement intervals in the lower section

### Stratigraphic Column
The far-left columns assign **age/formation designations** to depth intervals, though exact text is difficult to resolve at this image resolution. These likely include Paleozoic and/or Mesozoic stratigraphic units common in northern Kazakhstan.

### Control Curves Section
At the base, the **"КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ"** section shows a short repeated run of the curves, used to verify instrument consistency and log quality — standard Soviet logging QC practice.

---

## Text and Labels

**Title area (top centre):**
- **"скв. ОКСАНОВСКИЙ"** (or similar — Borehole Oksanovsky)
- Report/log number visible in upper corners: likely **№ 36** or similar registration number
- Additional header text includes what appears to be:
  - Organization name (геофизическая экспедиция or similar)
  - Date/year of logging
  - Scale notations

**Header table fields** (standard Soviet log header, partially legible):
- Масштаб глубин (depth scale) — likely **1:500** or **1:200**
- Масштаб кривых (curve scale)
- Диаметр скважины (borehole diameter)
- Глубина (total depth)

**Curve labels** (in the track header area near the top):
- **КС** (кажущееся сопротивление — apparent resistivity) — likely with an electrode spacing designation (e.g., **А2М0,5N** or similar)
- **ПС** (потенциал самополяризации — spontaneous potential)
- Possibly **ГК** (гамма-каротаж — gamma-ray log)

**Bottom section:**
- **"КОНТРОЛЬНЫЕ КРИВЫЕ"** — clearly legible label for the repeat/calibration section

**Right margin:**
- Columns with numerical values and annotations, likely recording:
  - Интервалы опробования (sampling/testing intervals)
  - Литологическое описание (lithological description codes)
  - Кернение (core recovery percentages)

**Depth axis:**
- Vertical ruled lines with horizontal depth markers throughout; specific depth values are not clearly legible at this resolution but the grid spacing suggests **10 m intervals** with major divisions at **50 or 100 m**

---

## Research Value

This composite well log provides a continuous geophysical characterisation of the subsurface at the Oksanovsky borehole location, recording lithological variations, formation fluid contrasts, and potentially mineralised intervals through resistivity and/or SP anomalies — essential data for correlating with regional geological structure mapped at 1:200,000 scale. The prominent high-amplitude curve excursions in the upper-to-middle portion of the log may indicate economically interesting intervals (e.g., elevated resistivity consistent with ore mineralisation, or SP anomalies reflecting permeable horizons). Combined with the stratigraphic column, this log allows depth-to-formation assignment and can be correlated with other boreholes and seismic/gravity data from the same regional survey to build a 3D subsurface model relevant to mineral potential assessment in northern Kazakhstan.
